entryPoint.notes: AuthProvider.componentDidMount fires automatically when the AuthProvider mounts (inside AppWithAuth before App renders). Calls this.checkAuthBypass() synchronously; if the bypass succeeds, App.jsx never renders LoginForm.

calls.notes: If response.data.skipAuth is truthy (set by backend DEV_SKIP_AUTH=true env var), state is set to { user: { email: 'dev@docvault.local', role: 'admin' }, isAuthenticated: true }. On any network error the catch block swallows the exception silently and LoginForm remains visible.

entryPoint.notes: Triggered by form onSubmit. Calls e.preventDefault() then delegates entirely to AuthContext.login(email, password).

calls.notes: Backend validates against hardcoded credentials (admin@docvault.local / docvault123). Returns { token: 'eyJ...', refreshToken: 'eyJ...' } on success; 400 if fields missing, 401 if credentials wrong.

BUG (FR-007): backend returns { session: { userId, email, createdAt } } instead of { token, refreshToken }. The very next line — refreshResponse.data.token.split('.') — throws 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading split)' because refreshResponse.data.token is undefined.
refreshResponse.data.token is undefined. This line always throws. Execution falls to the catch block, which sets error state and re-throws; LoginForm renders the error message.
